description: Create a two-column content slide titled 'Key actions for CROs' with explanatory text on the left and a radial process-style diagram showing the top 10 actions for CROs on the right.

header.kicker: SECOND ANNUAL EY/IF GLOBAL INSURANCE RISK MANAGEMENT SURVEY
header.headline: Key actions for CROs

left.body:
Our survey results suggest that this powerful combination of capabilities is what it will take to establish high-performing risk management functions in the future. In the meantime, CROs can prioritize the following actions.

Given all the uncertainty in the market today and the likelihood of more disruptive change, CROs must take a holistic approach in boosting the effectiveness of current risk management techniques. The big-picture objective is to ensure risk teams can deliver maximum strategic value and that risk management is viewed as a resource for achieving key goals.

steps.label: Prioritize relationship-building with senior leaders and the board
steps.sub: Facilitate information sharing, better understand business needs, and embed risk management in strategic planning relative to growth and innovation strategies, deployments of new technology and allocation capital

steps.label: Drive a stronger risk culture
steps.sub: Educate first-line business leaders, functional executives and the board on the latest risks and leading practices

steps.label: Expand the aperture of scenario planning and stress testing programs
steps.sub: Push participants to envision extreme, even unthinkable developments in terms of geopolitical conflict, macroeconomic shocks, cyber breaches and war-like disasters, including 'everything happening at once' situations

steps.label: Embrace a continuous improvement mindset in technology and data management
steps.sub: Enhance capabilities in pursuit of increased efficiency and seek opportunities to self-disrupt with technology

steps.label: Apply risk-based principles across core risk management tasks and activities
steps.sub: Ensure the right people and teams focus on the right risks at the right time, and design plans with resilience in mind through a balance of monitoring, prevention and incident response

steps.label: Proactively monitor regulatory developments
steps.sub: Use AI-powered scanning tools, participate in industry forums and engage directly with authorities to shape rulemaking, new requirements and industry standards

steps.label: Continually revisit and refine governance models for GenAI
steps.sub: Reflect priority use cases and new deployments while ensuring ongoing flexibility
